TWO CORRECTIONS to the finding, which is why I downgrade P2->P3 and rewrite the fix:

(1) The finding's "infinite-rebuild" claim is mechanically FALSE. I traced the engine (atlas.mjs). For S7/multi-phase, runInitiative:464-471 only honors a routeBack of targetStep:'S0'; a routeBack:S4 from S7 is NOT re-entered -- it falls through to line 471 and degrades to an "unhandled final-gate route-back" blocker. The true harm is a FALSE-NEGATIVE: an honest external-unavailable slice that correctly PASSED S4/S5 gets spuriously FAILed at attestation and either burns bounded re-entry budget on S4 re-builds that provably cannot yield a passing test (S6) or is immediately converted into a degraded blocker (S7) -- turning a legitimate ship-with-residual-risk outcome into a failure. (2) The contradiction is partly self-mitigating: both clause (b)s already append "(the same one deliverable S4 committed and S5 confirmed)", and for an external-unavailable slice that "same one" IS the skip test. verdicts.reasoning:
REJECT. The claimed terminology conflict between clause 6 (web-backend.md:111-115) and the two-axis `environment-scoped` model does not exist in the actual text; the finding's "could read as conflicting" depends on dropping two load-bearing qualifiers.

Clause 6 makes two propositions:
(1) Requirement: "the DESIGNATED dev DB/branch ... must be confirmed RUNNABLE and the behavior verified ON IT â€” the migration applied through the tool, the app boots, **the slice's path runs against that DB**." This is scoped to the DEV-env click-through activity (the "browser/user-flow R2 -> DEV env" axis). It never says the *automated-test* activity must run on the designated DB.
(2) Prohibition: "Verifying against the test/CI DB **while the designated DB stays broken** (un-migrated, drifted, or left mid-failure) is a FAIL." The conditioning clause "while the designated DB stays broken" makes this verbatim the `designated-env-broken` / `no_substitution` case (honest-verification.md:64 "no switching to a working branch because the designated one is broken"). It forbids using a test/CI-DB green as a substitute for an abandoned-broken designated DB â€” NOT running automated tests legitimately in the TEST env.

verdicts.reasoning:
REJECTED. The cross-reference facts are all true, but the finding's load-bearing materiality claims are false, so the defect as described is not real.

VERIFIED-TRUE (structure): grill-idea/SKILL.md line 97-98 marks `## Environments` MANDATORY for persistence/migration/external/UI surfaces; the launch-blocker gate (lines 118-126) clauses (i)+(ii) cover Surface Rulings + Required Capabilities only and omit `## Environments`; atlas.mjs line 425 PREFLIGHT FLOOR gates `## Surface Rulings` + `## Required Capabilities & Preflight` and does not enforce a non-empty `## Environments`; the `decisions` arg recipe (SKILL.md line 111) does not include `## Environments`; and S5 Â§6.2 (lines 201-218), web-backend.md clause 6 (lines 112-115, 200), s6 line 165, s7 line 149 all read `idea-decisions.md "## Environments"`.

FALSE (the harm): The finding claims "the build has the env designation NOWHERE," "There is no defined fallback in s5-verify-build.md or honest-verification.md for a missing `## Environments`," and that the autonomous build is "silently expected to invent" the env. All three are wrong:
1. A fallback IS defined at the front door: SKILL.md line 98 ends "...whenever a persistence/migration/external/UI surface is present; OTHERWISE STATE THE REPO DEFAULT." Non-domain launches resolve to the repo default, not nowhere.
2. A fallback IS defined in the consumer for an unresolved designated env. honest-verification.md lines 70-85 (blocker classification) + S5 Â§6.2 lines 212-218 explicitly forbid inventing/switching/mutating an env (that is a tamper â†’ automatic `environment-scoped` FAIL) and route a missing env-config to `config-resolvable` â†’ `verdict:BLOCKED, failedCriterion:R2_blocked_missing_config` naming the exact missing item, or `designated-env-broken` â†’ FAIL/BLOCKED. The engine then records the blocker and degrades gracefully (DESIGN-conformant hands-off behavior). So a domain-surface launch that skipped `## Environments` does NOT yield a silent-wrong/degraded ship â€” it yields a corroborated, named BLOCKED with the same teeth the proposed fix would add, already present downstream. The autonomous build cannot silently invent the env; the discipline makes invention a FAIL.

Because the silent-failure scenario the finding describes cannot occur, the P2 defect is not real. The only residual value in the proposed fix is efficiency/altitude â€” moving an already-guaranteed BLOCKED from S5 (after spec/slice/plan/build spend) earlier to the front door. But (a) the grill already marks `## Environments` MANDATORY for domain surfaces (SKILL.md line 97), so clause (iii) largely re-states an existing obligation, and (b) the engine-side PREFLIGHT addition duplicates the downstream `environment-scoped`/`config-resolvable` gate that already has teeth. Per the honor-lean bar (DESIGN Â§11: teeth not ceremony, no doubled gates), adding a second gate for a condition that is already MANDATORY at the front door AND already fail-safe downstream is ceremony, not a closed silent-wrong hole. Default-reject stands: the defect is mischaracterized and immaterial.

Mechanism verified against actual code/text. (1) The "just go" floor (atlas.md lines 29-30) gates the lean path entirely on refine's "Detected Surfaces = none." (2) The build's S0 preflight floor (atlas.mjs lines 424-426) keys off the SAME field â€” it BLOCKs only when idea-decisions.md lists "Detected Surfaces other than 'none'" with empty Surface Rulings. So if refine under-detects a one-way surface at the pre-code idea stage, neither the front-door floor nor S0 preflight catches it. (3) The build does discover it late: DESIGN Â§4 allows S2/S4 to promote a missed surface to CRITICAL, and corroborate() (mjs 250-264) force-escalates any one-way surface to Tier 3, building a reversible interim + ADR. (4) That un-ruled assumption surfaces only at the end via absorb() -> report.assumptions/obstructions/adrs. So the residual the finding describes is real and the user's intent for the missed surface is genuinely never captured.

This is NOT a dead-vestige issue (no new launch-arg, no unread return field â€” the proposed fix is prose-only and atlas.md runs in the conversational agent, not the sandbox, so sandbox-validity is moot).

Materiality: the residual itself is unavoidable and already correctly + transparently handled. The MATERIAL defect is the false reassurance in the existing prose. Lines 32-34 frame locking detected one-way surfaces as what makes "just go" safe, implying "Detected Surfaces = none" means "no one-way doors exist." It actually means only "refine spotted none" â€” refine's fallible idea-stage detection is the single point of failure for the lean path, and the current text never warns the user of that. A short warning gives the user a concrete reason to decline "just go" on a sensitive-domain idea even when refine flagged nothing â€” that changes user behavior (teeth, not ceremony), so it clears DESIGN Â§11's leanness bar ("if a sentence doesn't change what the agent/user does, delete it"). Confirmed at P3/low per the finding's own grading.
verdicts.refinedFix: Append one sentence to the floor paragraph at the end of atlas.md (after line 34): "Note that `Detected Surfaces = none` means refine *spotted* none from the idea â€” not that none exist; refine's pre-code detection is the only thing gating 'just go.' If the idea touches a sensitive domain (money | auth | data | migrations | external | PII | destructive), do NOT take the lean path on the strength of a clean detection alone â€” a surface refine misses gets no Surface Ruling, and the build will silently proceed on a reversible interim that may diverge from your intent, visible only in the final report." This adds no gate (a pre-code refine cannot reliably satisfy one) â€” it gives the user a decision-changing reason to keep full grill when the domain is sensitive even if refine flagged nothing.

synthesis.overall: NOT ready for dogfood: three confirmed P1 defects remain, all in the engine's S0 front-door floor and S0 re-entry logic (.claude/workflows/atlas.mjs), and every P1 blocks. The hardened S0 PREFLIGHT FLOOR â€” the only engine-side teeth against a direct launch that skipped grill â€” is currently largely ceremonial: it keys its primary trigger off a "## Detected Surfaces" section that does not exist in the file it names (idea-decisions.md; that field actually lives in refined-idea.md per the refine template), it is structurally blind to the absent-log case it was built for (a bypass produces no idea-decisions.md, so the condition is vacuously false and S0 writes a spec for money/auth/migration surfaces with zero rulings), and a runtime-failing front-door prerequisite drives an unconditional S0 route-back that re-runs S0 against an idea-decisions.md the engine never writes â€” a guaranteed no-op loop that burns the maxUpstreamRouteBacks budget. EFC-01 and LNC-01 both rewrite the same line (atlas.mjs:425) and must be applied as one coherent re-anchored floor (source surfaces from refined-idea.md "## Detected Surfaces" AND args, cross-check idea-decisions.md "## Surface Rulings" + "## Required Capabilities & Preflight", and treat an absent/unparseable log as itself BLOCKING when a domain surface is named); ASF-1 is a separate engine-loop guard. All fixes are sandbox-valid (prose/JS reusing already-read sources and the existing verdict:BLOCKED + routeBack envelope â€” no new launch-args, no new return fields). The five P2s (step-file floor contradiction, three dead-vestige envelope assertions / route-back doc mismatches, the self-declared-unreachable verifier dodge, the single-repo-default env false-FAIL) and five P3s are real polish that sharpen teeth and kill vestiges but do not block a run; fix them after the P1 floor is sound. Recommendation: land the three P1 engine fixes (one merged line-425 rewrite plus the ASF-1 helper), then dogfood.
